pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


DENVER, March 16.-The de
Tense fund of the Western Fedora
ton of Miners for the trial OF
Charles Meyer, William Haywood
and George Pettibone, accused OF
the assassination of formerGover'
nor Steunenberg, of Idaho, amount
ed to 0S6,S37,75 March I. This first
official and public announcement is
made by James Kirwan, secretary
treasurer of the union.


To March l there had been ex.
pended s73,500.15 in defending
Meyer. Haywood and Pettibone
and Steve Adams, whose case is re-
lated to the parent case, and
$685. L85 in defending Vincent St
John, whose case was involved in
the parent case and which has
been closed by dismissal.


The first contribution to the de-
Tense fund since it was started
soon after the arrest of Meyer and
Haywood in February, 1906, has
been with the most thorough and
systematic business methods in the
office of Acting Secretary-Treasurer
Kirwan An inspection of the &C
counts Of the fund was recently
made by Kirwan before represent
tative Of this newspaper, in behalf
of the many contributors. There IS
in preparation an itemized state
ment for each contributor.


BY the time the Moyer-Hay-
woodPettibone trial begins Kir'
wan believes the fund will have
reached more than 100000. Ktr-
wan also has assurances from
many labor unions that more funds
will be forthcoming If needed.
